---
title: "Influenza in Madagascar and Africa"
subtitle: "GISAID EpiFlu Metadata Analysis"
author: "EDID — Institut Pasteur de Madagascar"
date: today
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    output-file: "index.html"
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-title: "Contents"
    number-sections: true
    theme: cosmo
    fig-width: 10
    fig-height: 6
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
#| label: setup

from pathlib import Path
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.graph_objects as go

DATA_DIR = Path("../data")

df = pd.read_csv(DATA_DIR / "combined_metadata.tsv", sep="\t", parse_dates=["Collection_Date"])

africa = df[df["Continent"] == "Africa"].copy()
mdg    = df[df["Madagascar"] == True].copy()
rest   = df[(df["Continent"] == "Africa") & (df["Madagascar"] == False)].copy()

for d in [df, africa, mdg, rest]:
    d["Subtype"] = d["Subtype"].fillna("Unknown")

PALETTE    = px.colors.qualitative.Bold
MDG_COLOR  = PALETTE[1]
REST_COLOR = PALETTE[5]
```

## Overview

This report summarises all influenza sequences available in
[GISAID EpiFlu](https://gisaid.org/epiflu-applications/epiflu-database/)
for **Africa (all hosts, all types)** with a focus on **Madagascar**.
Data were downloaded on **16 March 2026**.

Genome completeness is defined at two levels:

- **Complete metadata**: all 8 segment accession IDs present in GISAID (`Complete_metadata`)
- **Phylo-ready**: all 8 segments have a sequence AND each segment meets minimum length thresholds for phylogenetics (PB2/PB1 ≥ 1,800 nt, PA ≥ 1,700, HA ≥ 1,300, NP ≥ 1,200, NA ≥ 1,000, MP ≥ 800, NS ≥ 700)

The two GISAID downloads are merged and deduplicated on `Isolate_Id`, with the most specific download taking precedence.

```{python}
#| label: tbl-overview

def completeness_row(label, d):
    n    = len(d)
    cm   = int(d["Complete_metadata"].sum()) if "Complete_metadata" in d.columns else 0
    pr   = int(d["phylo_ready"].sum()) if "phylo_ready" in d.columns else 0
    loc  = int(d["Country"].notna().sum())
    yr   = int(d["Year"].between(1990, 2026).sum())
    all3 = int((d["phylo_ready"] & d["Country"].notna() & d["Year"].between(1990, 2026)).sum()) if "phylo_ready" in d.columns else 0
    dr_min = d["Collection_Date"].min()
    dr_max = d["Collection_Date"].max()
    date_range = (
        f"{dr_min.date()} → {dr_max.date()}"
        if pd.notna(dr_min) and pd.notna(dr_max) else "—"
    )
    return {
        "Dataset": label,
        "Isolates": f"{n:,}",
        "Complete metadata": f"{cm:,} ({cm/n*100:.0f}%)",
        "Phylo-ready": f"{pr:,} ({pr/n*100:.0f}%)",
        "Location known": f"{loc:,} ({loc/n*100:.0f}%)",
        "Sampling date": f"{yr:,} ({yr/n*100:.0f}%)",
        "All criteria (phylo+loc+date)": f"{all3:,} ({all3/n*100:.0f}%)",
        "Date range": date_range,
    }

overview = pd.DataFrame([
    completeness_row("Madagascar", mdg),
    completeness_row("Rest of Africa", rest),
    completeness_row("All Africa", africa),
])

overview.to_html(index=False, classes="table table-sm table-bordered", border=0)
```

---

## Africa — All hosts

### Isolates per country

```{python}
#| label: fig-africa-map

country_counts = (
    africa.groupby("Country")
    .agg(
        Isolates=("Isolate_Id", "count"),
        Complete=("Complete_metadata", "sum"),
        Phylo=("phylo_ready", "sum"),
    )
    .reset_index()
)
country_counts["Completeness_pct"] = (
    country_counts["Complete"] / country_counts["Isolates"] * 100
).round(1)
country_counts["Phylo_pct"] = (
    country_counts["Phylo"] / country_counts["Isolates"] * 100
).round(1)

fig = px.choropleth(
    country_counts,
    locations="Country",
    locationmode="country names",
    color="Isolates",
    hover_name="Country",
    hover_data={"Isolates": True, "Completeness_pct": True, "Phylo_pct": True, "Country": False},
    color_continuous_scale="YlOrRd",
    scope="africa",
    title="Influenza isolates per African country — all hosts (GISAID EpiFlu)",
    labels={"Completeness_pct": "Complete genomes (%)"},
)
fig.update_geos(bgcolor="lightgrey", showcountries=True, countrycolor="white")
fig.show()
```

### Isolates per country — bar chart (top 15)

```{python}
#| label: fig-africa-bar

top15 = country_counts.nlargest(15, "Isolates").sort_values("Isolates", ascending=True)
top15["Color"] = top15["Country"].apply(lambda c: MDG_COLOR if c == "Madagascar" else REST_COLOR)

fig = go.Figure(go.Bar(
    x=top15["Isolates"],
    y=top15["Country"],
    orientation="h",
    marker_color=list(top15["Color"]),
    text=top15["Isolates"],
    textposition="outside",
))
fig.update_layout(
    title="Top 15 African countries by influenza isolate count — all hosts",
    xaxis_title="Isolates",
    yaxis_title="",
    showlegend=False,
    height=480,
)
fig.show()
```

### Sequences over time (Africa)

```{python}
#| label: fig-africa-timeline

af_t = africa[africa["Year"].between(1995, 2026)].copy()
top_sub = af_t["Subtype"].value_counts().head(7).index.tolist()
af_t["Subtype_grp"] = af_t["Subtype"].apply(lambda s: s if s in top_sub else "Other")

counts = af_t.groupby(["Year", "Subtype_grp"]).size().reset_index(name="n")

fig = px.bar(
    counts,
    x="Year", y="n",
    color="Subtype_grp",
    barmode="stack",
    color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    labels={"n": "Isolates", "Subtype_grp": "Subtype"},
    title="Africa — influenza isolates per year by subtype (all hosts)",
)
fig.update_layout(legend_title_text="Subtype")
fig.show()
```

### Host distribution (Africa)

```{python}
#| label: fig-africa-host

host_counts = africa["Host"].value_counts().reset_index()
host_counts.columns = ["Host", "n"]

fig = px.bar(
    host_counts,
    x="Host", y="n",
    color="Host",
    color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    text_auto=True,
    title="Africa — isolates by host (all hosts)",
    labels={"n": "Isolates"},
)
fig.update_layout(showlegend=False)
fig.show()
```

### Subtype distribution (Africa)

```{python}
#| label: fig-africa-subtype

top_sub_a = africa["Subtype"].value_counts().head(10).index.tolist()
africa["Subtype_grp"] = africa["Subtype"].apply(lambda s: s if s in top_sub_a else "Other")
sub_counts = africa["Subtype_grp"].value_counts().reset_index()
sub_counts.columns = ["Subtype", "n"]

fig = px.bar(
    sub_counts.sort_values("n", ascending=False),
    x="Subtype", y="n",
    color="Subtype",
    color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    text_auto=True,
    title="Africa — isolates by subtype (all hosts)",
    labels={"n": "Isolates"},
)
fig.update_layout(showlegend=False)
fig.show()
```

### Genome completeness by country (top 10)

```{python}
#| label: fig-africa-comp-country

top10 = africa["Country"].value_counts().head(10).index.tolist()
af_t10 = africa[africa["Country"].isin(top10)].copy()

comp_c = (
    af_t10.groupby(["Country", "Complete_metadata"])
    .size().reset_index(name="n")
)
comp_c["Label"] = comp_c["Complete_metadata"].map({True: "Complete", False: "Partial"})

fig = px.bar(
    comp_c,
    x="Country", y="n",
    color="Label",
    color_discrete_map={"Complete": PALETTE[2], "Partial": PALETTE[5]},
    barmode="stack",
    category_orders={"Country": top10},
    labels={"n": "Isolates", "Label": "Genome"},
    title="Africa — genome completeness by country (top 10)",
)
fig.show()
```

---

## Madagascar

### Host distribution

```{python}
#| label: fig-mdg-host

host_mdg = mdg["Host"].value_counts().reset_index()
host_mdg.columns = ["Host", "n"]

fig = px.bar(
    host_mdg,
    x="Host", y="n",
    color="Host",
    color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    text_auto=True,
    title="Madagascar — isolates by host",
    labels={"n": "Isolates"},
)
fig.update_layout(showlegend=False)
fig.show()
```

### Sequences over time by subtype

```{python}
#| label: fig-mdg-timeline

mdg_t = mdg[mdg["Year"].between(1990, 2026)].copy()
top_st = mdg_t["Subtype"].value_counts().head(6).index.tolist()
mdg_t["Subtype_grp"] = mdg_t["Subtype"].apply(lambda s: s if s in top_st else "Other")

counts_m = mdg_t.groupby(["Year", "Subtype_grp"]).size().reset_index(name="n")

fig = px.bar(
    counts_m,
    x="Year", y="n",
    color="Subtype_grp",
    barmode="stack",
    color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    labels={"n": "Isolates", "Subtype_grp": "Subtype"},
    title="Madagascar — influenza isolates per year by subtype",
)
fig.update_layout(legend_title_text="Subtype", xaxis_title="Collection year")
fig.show()
```

### Subtype distribution

```{python}
#| label: fig-mdg-subtype

top_sub_m = mdg["Subtype"].value_counts().head(10).index.tolist()
mdg["Subtype_grp"] = mdg["Subtype"].apply(lambda s: s if s in top_sub_m else "Other")
sub_m = mdg["Subtype_grp"].value_counts().reset_index()
sub_m.columns = ["Subtype", "n"]

fig = px.bar(
    sub_m.sort_values("n", ascending=False),
    x="Subtype", y="n",
    color="Subtype",
    color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    text_auto=True,
    title="Madagascar — isolates by subtype",
    labels={"n": "Isolates"},
)
fig.update_layout(showlegend=False)
fig.show()
```

### Genome completeness

```{python}
#| label: fig-mdg-completeness

comp_m = mdg.groupby("Complete_metadata").size().reset_index(name="n")
comp_m["Label"] = comp_m["Complete_metadata"].map(
    {True: "Complete (8/8 segments)", False: "Partial"}
)

fig = px.pie(
    comp_m,
    names="Label", values="n",
    color_discrete_sequence=[PALETTE[2], PALETTE[5]],
    title="Madagascar — genome completeness",
    hole=0.4,
)
fig.show()
```

### Completeness by subtype (top 6)

```{python}
#| label: fig-mdg-comp-subtype

top6_m = mdg["Subtype"].value_counts().head(6).index.tolist()
mdg_top = mdg[mdg["Subtype"].isin(top6_m)].copy()

fig = px.histogram(
    mdg_top,
    x="Subtype",
    color="Complete_metadata",
    barmode="stack",
    color_discrete_map={True: PALETTE[2], False: PALETTE[5]},
    labels={"Complete_metadata": "Complete genome"},
    title="Madagascar — completeness by subtype (top 6)",
    category_orders={"Subtype": top6_m},
)
fig.update_layout(yaxis_title="Isolates", xaxis_title="Subtype")
fig.show()
```

---

## Sequence Quality

For each isolate with sequences, we computed the length of every segment
available in the FASTA files. This section goes beyond metadata completeness
to show whether segments are long enough for phylogenetic analysis.

### Segment length distribution (Africa, complete genomes)

```{python}
#| label: fig-seg-lengths-africa

SEGMENTS = ["PB2", "PB1", "PA", "HA", "NP", "NA", "MP", "NS"]
MIN_LEN = {"PB2": 1800, "PB1": 1800, "PA": 1700,
            "HA": 1300, "NP": 1200, "NA": 1000, "MP": 800, "NS": 700}

# Only isolates with at least one sequence
af_seq = africa[africa["n_segs_sequenced"] > 0].copy()

# Melt to long format
length_cols = [f"{s}_length" for s in SEGMENTS if f"{s}_length" in af_seq.columns]
long = af_seq[["Isolate_Id", "Country"] + length_cols].melt(
    id_vars=["Isolate_Id", "Country"],
    var_name="Segment", value_name="Length"
).dropna(subset=["Length"])
long["Segment"] = long["Segment"].str.replace("_length", "")

# Add minimum-length threshold line
thresholds = pd.DataFrame([
    {"Segment": s, "Min_length": v} for s, v in MIN_LEN.items()
])

fig = px.box(
    long,
    x="Segment", y="Length",
    color="Segment",
    color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    category_orders={"Segment": SEGMENTS},
    title="Africa — segment length distribution (all isolates with sequences)",
    labels={"Length": "Sequence length (nt)"},
    points=False,
)
# Add min-length threshold markers
for _, row in thresholds.iterrows():
    fig.add_shape(type="line",
        xref="x", yref="y",
        x0=row["Segment"], x1=row["Segment"],
        y0=row["Min_length"] - 30, y1=row["Min_length"] + 30,
        line=dict(color="red", width=3))
fig.add_scatter(x=[None], y=[None], mode="markers",
    marker=dict(color="red", size=8, symbol="line-ew"),
    name="Min length for phylogenetics")
fig.show()
```

### Phylo-ready isolates per country (top 10)

```{python}
#| label: fig-phylo-country

top10_p = africa["Country"].value_counts().head(10).index.tolist()
af_p = africa[africa["Country"].isin(top10_p)].copy()

comp_p = (
    af_p.groupby(["Country", "phylo_ready"])
    .size().reset_index(name="n")
)
comp_p["Label"] = comp_p["phylo_ready"].map({True: "Phylo-ready", False: "Not phylo-ready"})

fig = px.bar(
    comp_p,
    x="Country", y="n",
    color="Label",
    color_discrete_map={"Phylo-ready": PALETTE[2], "Not phylo-ready": PALETTE[5]},
    barmode="stack",
    category_orders={"Country": top10_p},
    labels={"n": "Isolates", "Label": "Status"},
    title="Africa — phylo-ready isolates by country (top 10)",
)
fig.show()
```

### Segment coverage heatmap (Madagascar)

Each column is one Madagascar isolate that has at least one sequence in the
FASTA files, sorted left-to-right from most complete (8 segments) to least
complete (1 segment). Each row is one of the 8 core segments. A coloured cell
means the sequence for that segment was downloaded; a grey cell means it is
absent. This lets you see at a glance which segments are most often missing
and whether certain isolates are missing a consistent set.

```{python}
#| label: fig-mdg-seg-heatmap

SEGMENTS = ["PB2", "PB1", "PA", "HA", "NP", "NA", "MP", "NS"]
length_cols_mdg = [f"{s}_length" for s in SEGMENTS if f"{s}_length" in mdg.columns]

# Only isolates with at least one sequence, deduplicated on Isolate_Id
heat = mdg[mdg["n_segs_sequenced"] > 0][
    ["Isolate_Id", "Subtype", "Year", "n_segs_sequenced"] + length_cols_mdg
].drop_duplicates("Isolate_Id").copy()
heat = heat.sort_values("n_segs_sequenced", ascending=False)
heat_bin = heat[length_cols_mdg].notna().astype(int)
heat_bin.columns = SEGMENTS
heat_bin.index = heat["Isolate_Id"].values

fig = px.imshow(
    heat_bin.T,
    color_continuous_scale=[[0, PALETTE[5]], [1, PALETTE[2]]],
    title="Madagascar — segment presence per isolate (sorted by completeness)",
    labels={"x": "Isolate", "y": "Segment", "color": "Present"},
    aspect="auto",
)
fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_showticklabels=False,
    coloraxis_showscale=False,
    yaxis_title="Segment",
)
fig.show()
```

---

## Data Sources

- **GISAID EpiFlu** (<https://gisaid.org>) — data downloaded on **16 March 2026**

- Madagascar dataset: all hosts, all influenza types.
- Africa dataset: all hosts, all African countries, all influenza types.
